title: Dryostachyum speciosum
intro: Unfortunately, I was unable to locate any information on the plant 'Dryostachyum speciosum'.
taxonomy: Dryostachyum speciosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is native to New Zealand and is considered an endangered species due to its limited range and habitat loss. The classification of Dryostachyum speciosum is further detailed within the subfamily Apioideae, which comprises many other species of plants. More specifically, it belongs to the tribe Apieae and the genus Dryostachyum. Further research is needed to understand the full implications of its classification.
